text.title: audience-segmentation Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for audience-segmentation as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Producing eight micro-segments using five dimensions without a cap.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Segments beyond four become unmanageable; force a two to three dimension cap and merge the long tail into a do-not-target group.

failure-mode.description: Demographic-only segmentation (age, gender, location) for a B2B product.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Demographics predict lookup, not purchase; B2B requires industry, employee count, and role as primary axes, with needs-based dimensions second.

failure-mode.description: Behavioural-clustering agent over-fits to one outlier whale customer, creating a segment of one.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require feature standardisation and scree-plot evidence for k before accepting cluster results.

failure-mode.description: Reverse-ETL bot syncing segments to ad platforms without an approval gate.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents merging segments to paid media without a kill switch can exhaust ad budgets in hours; require human approval before production activation.
